demographics.living_situation: lives in a supported group residential home; usually needs minimal assistance for daily living

contact_nok.relationship: support worker (present; calling on the patient's behalf)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Support worker calling) He won't look at us, he keeps hitting his wrists against the wall while flapping his hands, and he's hyperventilating in the corner. He has Fragile X and usually copes okay, but a new staff member started this evening and about four hours ago he just started escalating and we can't settle him with the things that normally work.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He is hurting himself and we can't calm him down after hours of trying — we need help now and we can't wait until the morning.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 4 hours ago, shortly after a new, unfamiliar staff member started the evening shift
symptom_narrative.character: won't make eye contact, extreme anxiety, pacing, then rapid forceful hand-flapping and striking his wrists against the wall and furniture; hyperventilating
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: worse than his usual episodes — nothing we normally do is working and he's starting to hurt his wrists
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: he keeps banging both wrists; they look red and a bit swollen now
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: came on over the first hour after the new staff member arrived and has stayed severe since
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: usually a quiet space and his familiar routine settle him, but not tonight; he refused his PRN calming tablet
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the living room is crowded with staff right now and that seems to wind him up more
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: fast breathing, looks like his heart is racing, very distressed and won't be redirected
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has anxiety and meltdowns when his routine changes, but they usually settle with his normal strategies and don't get to self-harm like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely unable to do his usual evening routine; currently overwhelmed by panic

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: Fragile X syndrome (a genetic condition he was born with)
past_medical_history.how_managed: supported accommodation, behavioural strategies, familiar routines, allied-health input

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: intellectual disability — he needs support and cannot always tell us what is wrong
anxiety and sensory sensitivity, worse with change
past_medical_history.how_managed: de-escalation strategies, weighted blanket, social stories

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: clonidine — a PRN calming tablet kept for episodes like this

family_history_as_reported.narrative: Fragile X is inherited; support worker is not sure of the wider family details

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 22-year-old man in a supported group home; relies on support workers; routine and familiar staff are important to keeping him settled

communication_style.typical_message_style: history given entirely by the support worker; the patient himself is non-verbal in crisis and cannot self-report

access_barriers.other: patient cannot self-report symptoms; all information is proxy via the support worker, who does not have clinical training

objective_data_offered.value: He's striking both wrists against the wall and furniture while flapping his hands hard, won't make eye contact, and is hyperventilating in the corner
Both wrists look red and a little swollen where he's been hitting them; no obvious deformity that we can see
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: support-worker observation over video; not a clinical examination

objective_data_offered.value: He's breathing very fast and looks like his heart is racing; he seems flushed and distressed
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer impression only; no home device reading available

objective_data_offered.value: The living room is crowded with staff, which seems to be making him worse; a new unfamiliar worker started the shift tonight
